steps.title: Find JL using the midpoint condition
steps.content: Since $K$ is the midpoint of $\overline{JL}$, the two halves are equal: $JK = KL$. Given that $KL = 13$ units, we have $JL = JK + KL = 13 + 13 = 26$ units.

steps.title: Determine the radius using the semicircle's center
steps.content: The problem states that $\overline{LM}$ is a radius of semicircle $L$, meaning $L$ is the center. Since $\overline{JM}$ is the diameter, the center $L$ must be its midpoint. Therefore, the distance from the center to each endpoint equals the radius: $LM = JL = 26$ units.

steps.title: Calculate the rectangle dimensions and area
steps.content:
The rectangle has corners at $K$, $M$, $N$, and $O$. Its width is the distance along the top edge: $$KM = KL + LM = 13 + 26 = 39 \text{ units}$$
Its height is the right edge: $MN = 14$ units (given). The rectangle area is $$39 \times 14 = 546 \text{ square units}$$

steps.title: Calculate the semicircle area and total
steps.content:
The semicircle has radius $r = 26$, so its area is $$\frac{1}{2}\pi r^2 = \frac{1}{2}\pi(26)^2 = \frac{1}{2}\pi(676) = 338\pi \text{ square units}$$
The total area of the figure is $$546 + 338\pi \text{ square units}$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
